message:
fix(security): XSS prevention in simple HTML export and defensive string slicing

Three fixes found during cross-agent code audit:

1. XSS in format_as_html (src/lib.rs:13977): Session titles were
   interpolated directly into <title> and <h1> tags without escaping.
   A malicious session title like "<script>alert(1)</script>" would
   execute JavaScript when the exported HTML file was opened in a
   browser. Fixed by applying html_escape() to the title before
   interpolation. The main export-html pipeline (HtmlExporter) was
   already safe — this only affected the simpler `cass export` path.

2. Incomplete html_escape (src/lib.rs:14128): The html_escape function
   was missing single-quote escaping ('), creating an inconsistency
   with the more complete version in html_export/template.rs:770 which
   properly escapes all five HTML special characters. Added the missing
   .replace('\'', "'") for defense-in-depth.

3. Unchecked byte slicing on fingerprint strings (src/lib.rs:10297,
   src/pages/wizard.rs:1742): `fingerprint[..8]` would panic if the
   fingerprint were ever shorter than 8 bytes. While SHA-256 hex output
   is always 64+ chars, this is brittle coupling to an implementation
   detail. Changed to .get(..8).unwrap_or(&fingerprint) for safe
   degradation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
